questions.statement: &nbsp;Which player was selected as the &quot;Player of the Match&quot; in both the 2017 and 2025 IPL finals?
India&rsquo;s first Bharat Ratna Dr Babasaheb Ambedkar Advocate Training and Research Centre (BBATRC) was inaugurated at which of the following places?
The Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) organised the 2nd edition of the coastal cyclothon titled Vande Mataram Coastal Cyclothon 2026 with which theme?
<p>In India, which authority conducts the independent technical investigation of civil aircraft accidents?</p>
options: Directorate General of Civil Aviation, which regulates flight safety and airworthiness /
Airports Authority of India, which manages airports and air navigation services /
Bureau of Civil Aviation Security, which oversees aviation security measures /
Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau, which investigates air crashes /

questions.statement: <div><span style="">&#39;Cyclone Dana&#39;, which affected the coasts of Odisha and West Bengal in October 2024, was named by which country?</span></div>
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is Qatar.

Key Points

  • The name ‘Dana’ for the cyclone that affected Odisha and West Bengal in October 2024 was contributed by Qatar, as per the naming conventions established by the World Meteorological Organization (WMO).
  • The naming of cyclones in the northern Indian Ocean region, including the Bay of Bengal and the Arabian Sea, is handled by 13 countries, including Qatar, under the WMO/ESCAP panel.
  • Cyclone names are pre-decided and listed by the member countries; these names are then used sequentially.
  • The name ‘Dana’ was chosen from Qatar’s list of contributed names and was assigned based on the sequence in which cyclones occur.
  • Qatar’s contribution to cyclone naming reflects its active participation in the regional meteorological community, despite being geographically distant from the primary cyclone zones.

Additional Information

  • India
    • India is a member of the WMO/ESCAP panel and contributes cyclone names for the northern Indian Ocean region.
    • Some of India’s recent contributions to cyclone names include ‘Agni’, ‘Akash’, and ‘Gati’.
    • India Meteorological Department (IMD) is the nodal agency for issuing cyclone alerts and warnings in the Indian Ocean region.
  • Pakistan
    • Pakistan is also a member of the WMO/ESCAP panel and actively contributes cyclone names.
    • Names like ‘Nilofar’ and ‘Bulbul’ were suggested by Pakistan and used for cyclones in the past.
    • Pakistan’s contributions to cyclone naming highlight its cooperation in disaster mitigation and preparedness in the region.
  • Thailand
    • Thailand is a member of the WMO/ESCAP panel and contributes cyclone names for the region.
    • Some names contributed by Thailand include ‘Phailin’ and ‘Amphan’.
    • Thailand, like other member countries, plays an important role in regional disaster preparedness and response planning.
  • Cyclone Naming System
    • The practice of naming cyclones helps in better communication and creates public awareness about potential hazards.
    • The WMO/ESCAP panel for the northern Indian Ocean includes 13 countries: India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Maldives, Myanmar, Oman, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Yemen, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Iran, and the UAE.
    • Each country submits a list of names, which are used sequentially for naming cyclones as they form.
    • The naming system ensures uniformity and prevents confusion when multiple cyclones occur in the same region.
